subject: Anthropology
title: Health and Migration
description: A quick, practical guide to Health and Migration.

hook.text: A refugee reaches a new country and faces a new health system, a new language, and distrust. Migration reshapes health.

buildup.text: Migrants face unique health risks — trauma, discrimination, language barriers, and loss of familiar healing systems.

discovery.text: The healthy immigrant paradox shows new arrivals are often healthier than locals — but their health declines over time.

twist.text: Host countries blame migrants for disease. But the real health threat is the structural exclusion migrants face.

climax.text: Migration reveals that health is tied to belonging. Without inclusion, even the healthiest bodies break down.

quiz.question: What is the healthy immigrant paradox?
options: New arrivals are often healthier than locals but decline over time
